AI Workflow Automation

Aye is a Chromium-based AI browser for macOS and Windows that gives web work a teachable AI intern. It reads visible pages, plans steps, and works through normal browser actions: clicking, typing, scrolling, switching tabs, and checking results. Summarize pages, research across tabs, draft replies, and automate repeatable workflows. Turn recurring tasks into reusable skills, separate accounts with profiles, and stay in control with reviewable progress and approval for sensitive steps.

# Aye Review

Last modified: |  Fred Magni

Meet Aye, your digital sidekick for browser automation. Billed as your “teachable AI intern for everyday browser work,” Aye is a general-purpose AI browser that plans, acts, and verifies results directly on real websites. Now available for Mac and Windows, it’s designed to liberate you from the mundane.

Aye Review

Uniqueness 82%

The uniqueness score is 82%.

Ease of Use 80%

The ease of use score is 80%.

Visit Website  Have an AI Tool? Submit it here!

## Main Features

Aye excels at delegating browser chores, transforming repetitive tasks into streamlined workflows. Its core capabilities include: [...] ## Main Target

Aye is meticulously crafted for anyone seeking to reclaim time from browser-based drudgery. Its ideal users include:

 Digital Professionals: Content creators, social media managers, and support specialists burdened by repetitive tasks.
 Small Teams: Streamlining customer service, community management, and content distribution across multiple platforms.
 Productivity Enthusiasts: Individuals eager to automate routine digital tasks and optimize their digital workflows.

Stop browsing, start delegating. Download Aye today and transform how you interact with the web.

## Top Alternatives to Aye

Let’s explore and discover the best alternatives and similar tools to Aye, carefully selected and ranked based on functionality, reliability, and user experience. [...] Beyond individual tasks, Aye offers robust automation:

 Save as a Skill: Turn any successful task into a reusable “Skill,” eliminating repetitive instructions.
 Schedule Automation: Run your custom Skills daily, weekly, or at fixed intervals to keep routine work flowing.
 User-Centric Control: Aye operates transparently, pausing for your approval on critical actions (submissions, payments) and handing over for sign-ins or CAPTCHAs, ensuring you remain in control.

A glimpse into Aye’s delegation power:

| Task Type | Aye’s Action |
 --- |
| Video Downloads | Saves from X, Telegram, Reddit. |
| Email Support | Drafts natural, contextual replies. |
| Social Maintenance | Handles replies, notifications, spam. |
| Market Research | Compares tools, collects data. |

Frequently asked questions

## What you may want to know before starting.

How is Aye different from a regular AI chat tool?＋

Aye does more than generate answers. It understands the current page, acts on real websites, verifies results, and completes multi-step tasks.

 Can these workflows run automatically on a schedule?＋

Yes. Set a Prompt Skill or recorded Skill to run once, at intervals, daily, or weekly.

 Which platforms does Aye support?＋

Aye is available from the Mac App Store and Microsoft Store for Mac and Windows. [...] ↗

### Video and media downloads

Save videos you can access from X, Telegram, and Reddit, with download progress in one place.

✦

### Support email replies

Handle in-scope Gmail support messages and reply naturally in English or Chinese based on context.

✓

### Daily community maintenance

Handle replies, notifications, and spam across Reddit, Tieba, Xiaohongshu, and X.

↻

### Multi-platform content preparation

Prepare platform-native drafts for Xiaohongshu, Zhihu, WeChat, and X, ready for your review before publishing.

You stay in control

## Automation does not mean acting without permission.

Aye shows you what it is doing. It pauses for your input at sign-in, CAPTCHA, submission, payment, and other sensitive steps.

results.title: Top 5 Best browser use alternative alternatives for agencies
results.content:
## 1. Simular Pro – From Browser Agent to True AI Co‑Worker

If Browser Use feels like hiring a smart intern for your browser, Simular Pro is more like bringing on a full-time digital operator who can run your whole computer.

Simular’s computer-use agents (often referred to as Sai) work the way you do: they move the mouse, type into apps, operate GUIs, call APIs, use terminals, and even write and run code. Instead of being tied to a single browser tab, they live on a private, cloud-based virtual desktop that’s always on, isolated, and secure. You just describe the outcome—“clean my CRM, send follow-ups to warm leads, update the Notion report”—and your agent executes it end to end. [...] ‍

That’s why tools like Browser Use have exploded in popularity. Browser Use is an open‑source AI browser automation project that lets an LLM control a browser the way you would: clicking, typing, scrolling, and navigating pages. It can log in, fill forms, scrape complex sites, and chain multi‑step workflows from a natural‑language prompt, as covered in reviews from Skyvern’s 2025 guide and the AI Agents Directory. For many teams, it’s their first taste of delegating web work to an agent instead of a junior VA.

‍ [...] ‍

Pricing starts around $50/month and scales with usage.

For larger agencies or product companies with engineering muscle, Browserbase can be the backbone for custom browser agents. For non-technical teams, or those who want automation that also touches spreadsheets, Figma, local databases, or email clients, Simular Pro provides a far more opinionated, end-user-friendly stack.

‍

## 5. MultiOn – A Friendly Web Agent for Everyday Tasks

MultiOn sits closer to the consumer side of the spectrum: it’s a pre-built AI web agent you can ask to “book me a flight,” “summarize this article,” or “find and compare the best tools for X.” Underneath, it controls the browser autonomously to complete these tasks.

‍

Pros
